Off Calendar Jul-22-2025 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jul-22-2025 Continued To Oct-21-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Milla Lvovich, Esq., in person, for Yafim Ratinsky, Petitioner.
George Wailes, Esq., via Zoom videoconference, for Alexander Vaynberg, Objector.
Andrew Schultz, Esq., via Zoom videoconference, for Minna Fernan, interested Party.

The following matters are concurrently called: PTR-20-303912 and PES-24-3022295.

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions regarding the status of the Trust and related Estate matters. There is a request to expand Mr. Ratinski's powers which is opposed by Mr. Wailes. There is discussion of setting the Trust matter for trial and subsequently the status of Discovery. The court advises that it typically will not set a matter for trial until Discovery has been completed. After additional discussion, the status of mediation in the above matters are taken off calendar. The remaining subject matters in the Trust action are continued to Oct-21-2025 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. The subject matter in the Estate action is continued to Oct-21-2025 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. The court advises that it is taking the request to expand Mr. Ratinski's powers under submission with the court acknowledging Mr. Wailes' objections.

Judge: Hon. Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Nguyen. Not Reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Feb-05-2025 Continued To Apr-08-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard together:
PTR-20-303912, In the Matter of the Mikhail M. Vaynberg and Beatrisa Vaynberg Living Trust, and
PES-24-307295, The Estate of Mikhail Vaynberg

Appearances:
Milla L. Lvovich for Yafim Ratinski, Respondent (Trust Case) and Proposed Executor (Estate Case).
George Wailes for Alexander Vaynberg, Petitioner (Trust Case) and Objector (Estate Case).
Jeffrey Leow for Minna Fernan, Trustee of the B-trust.

The court acknowledged a Joint Status Report submitted for filing on 2/4/25, and questioned discrepancies regarding informal and formal discovery. Parties agreed to mediation with Betty Epstein, but a dispute arose regarding financial disclosures needed before mediation. Mr. Wailes raised concerns over further delays, while Ms. Lvovich argued that essential financial records had not been provided since 2022. The court expressed concerns about an unproductive mediation without an exchange of information.

The court informed counsel that a Breslin Order will be issued. Mediation is to be completed by May 7, 2025. Informal Discovery must be completed by March 5, 2025. A meet-and-confer session must occur to resolve any missing information or disputes.

In the estate matter, the court deferred ruling on the petition for ancillary probate. Ms. Lvovich was directed to obtain a certified copy of the will from Israel. The matter was continued to April 8, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, for further updates.

In the trust matter, the court set a status hearing regarding the status of mediation for May 14, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. The two instant petitions were also continued to be heard on the same date. Counsel was instructed by the court to notify the Probate Examiner by May 7, 2025, regarding mediation results.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)
